topic: Area of Study 4: Popular Music and Jazz
subject: music

items.q: What is a balanced antecedent-consequent phrase?
items.correct: Two phrases of equal length where the first sounds open (question) and the second resolves (answer).
items.misconception: Any two phrases of similar style.
items.random_wrong: A long melody with no breaks.
items.why: Students miss the FUNCTIONAL relationship. The antecedent typically ends on an imperfect cadence (open); the consequent answers with a perfect cadence (closed). Length matches; cadences contrast.

items.correct: A recurring main theme (A) alternates with contrasting episodes (B, C, etc.).
items.misconception: A round where voices enter at different times.
items.why: Students confuse rondo (alternating refrain) with round (canon). Rondo: ABACA, ABACABA, etc. The A theme keeps coming back, like a refrain.

items.correct: Two or more independent melodic lines sounding together.
items.misconception: A single melody with chord accompaniment.
items.why: Students confuse polyphony with thick texture or homophony. Polyphony specifically means INDEPENDENT MELODIES - like a fugue. Melody + chords is HOMOPHONY, not polyphony.

items.q: In what city was Motown Records founded in 1959?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Detroit. “New York” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which band's 1977 album 'Never Mind the Bollocks' is a landmark of British punk?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: The Sex Pistols. “The Clash” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year was Motown Records founded by Berry Gordy?
items.why: Exact answer: 1959. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
